title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Fashion is a (0) POWERFUL form of self-expression that changes with the seasons. Throughout history, clothing has been used to indicate (17) status and cultural identity. The (18) of mass-produced garments in the 20th century made trendy styles more (19) to the general public. However, the (20) of 'fast fashion' has raised concerns about (21). Many (22) are now focusing on eco-friendly materials to reduce waste. This (23) towards ethical fashion encourages consumers to value quality over quantity. (24), clothes were made to last for years, and a return to this mindset is seen as essential for the planet. By choosing timeless pieces and supporting brands that prioritize fair labor practices, individuals can create a wardrobe that is both stylish and responsible, proving that looking good does not have to come at a cost to the environment or the people who make our clothes.

example.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'form'; POWER → POWERFUL.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'status'; SOCIETY → SOCIAL.
Noun required after the definite article; APPEAR → APPEARANCE.
Plural noun for people required; DESIGN → DESIGNERS.
Adverb required at the start of the sentence; ORIGIN → ORIGINALLY.
